TEXT.citation: Early, R. J., & Whitehorn, J. (2003). One hundred Paiwan texts. Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University.
TEXT.BibTeX_citation: @book{100paiwantexts, author = {Robert Early and John Whitehorn}, title = {One Hundred Paiwan Texts}, year = {2003}, publisher = {Pacific Linguistics, Research School of Pacific and Asian Studies, The Australian National University}}
TEXT.source: told by Giɫgiɫaw of Vaɫulu village

S.FORM: aitsu a qam tjara kemɫang itjen a mapuɫat, aitsu a qam matsaqu aravats a kemali tua qipu, saka pazangal aravats a djameqen.
S.PHON: aiʦu a qam cara kəmʎaŋ icən a mapuʎat, aiʦu a qam maʦaqu aravaʦ a kəmaɭi tua qipu, saka pazaŋaɭ aravaʦ a ɉaməqən.
S.FORM: aicu a qam tjara kemljang itjen a mapuljat, aicu a qam macaqu aravac a kemali tua qipu, saka pazangal aravac a djameqen.
S.PHON: aiʦu a qam ʦara kəmʎaŋ iʦən a mapuʎat, aiʦu a qam maʦaqu aravaʦ a kəmaɭi tua qipu, saka pazaŋaɭ aravaʦ a ɉaməqən.
S.TRANSL: We all know about pangolins; they are very good at digging in the earth, and very hard to catch.

S.FORM: nu inika sitjara kiqam a tsautsau, inika djemamedjameq a paqeteleng.
S.PHON: nu inika sicara kiqam a ʦauʦau, inika ɉəmaməɉaməq a paqətəɭəŋ.
S.FORM: nu inika sitjara kiqam a caucau, inika djemamedjameq a paqeteleng.
S.PHON: nu inika siʦara kiqam a ʦauʦau, inika ɉəmaməɉaməq a paqətəɭəŋ.
S.TRANSL: People can’t catch them any old how without being specialists at catching them.

S.FORM: aitsu a qam tja keɫangen a mapuɫat tu rusiaqan; kana manu, sinipasan qam sun, ayaya itjen tua rusiaqan.
S.PHON: aiʦu a qam ca kəʎaŋən a mapuʎat tu rusiaqan; kana manu, sinipasan qam sun, ajaja icən tua rusiaqan.
S.FORM: aicu a qam tja keljangen a mapuljat tu rusiaqan; kana manu, sinipasan qam sun, ayaya itjen tua rusiaqan.
S.PHON: aiʦu a qam ʦa kəʎaŋən a mapuʎat tu rusiaqan; kana manu, sinipasan qam sun, ajaja iʦən tua rusiaqan.
S.TRANSL: We all know they are very shy. That’s why we say to a shy person: you’ve become a pangolin.

S.FORM: aitsu a qam sasiq a kakanen.
S.PHON: aiʦu a qam sasiq a kakanən.
S.FORM: aicu a qam sasiq a kakanen.
S.PHON: aiʦu a qam sasiq a kakanən.

S.FORM: saka nu patsun tua sasiq maligiɫ aravats.
S.PHON: saka nu paʦun tua sasiq maɭigiʎ aravaʦ.
S.FORM: saka nu pacun tua sasiq maligilj aravac.
S.PHON: saka nu paʦun tua sasiq maɭigiʎ aravaʦ.
S.TRANSL: When they see ants they are very clever;

S.FORM: kirimu a kembung timadju, sa tselaki aza qauɫivan nimadju, saka mimatsayan.
S.PHON: kirimu a kəmbuŋ timaɉu, sa ʦəɭaki aza qauʎivan nimaɉu, saka mimaʦajan.
S.FORM: kirimu a kembung timadju, sa celaki aza qauljivan nimadju, saka mimacayan.
S.PHON: kirimu a kəmbuŋ timaɉu, sa ʦəɭaki aza qauʎivan nimaɉu, saka mimaʦajan.
S.TRANSL: they quickly lie down and open their scales and pretend to be dead.

S.FORM: qau nu tevuta azua mareka sasiq a sema taladj tazua mareka qauɫivan, tsaqevan anga nimadju tazua qauɫivan nimadju; sa vaikan anga a sema tua zaɫum a tsemlak ta qauɫivan nimadju.
S.PHON: qau nu təvuta azua marəka sasiq a səma taɭaɉ tazua marəka qauʎivan, ʦaqəvan aŋa nimaɉu tazua qauʎivan nimaɉu; sa vaikan aŋa a səma tua zaʎum a ʦəmɭak ta qauʎivan nimaɉu.
S.FORM: qau nu tevuta azua mareka sasiq a sema taladj tazua mareka qauljivan, caqevan anga nimadju tazua qauljivan nimadju; sa vaikan anga a sema tua zaljum a cemlak ta qauljivan nimadju.
S.PHON: qau nu təvuta azua marəka sasiq a səma taɭaɉ tazua marəka qauʎivan, ʦaqəvan aŋa nimaɉu tazua qauʎivan nimaɉu; sa vaikan aŋa a səma tua zaʎum a ʦəmɭak ta qauʎivan nimaɉu.
S.TRANSL: When the ants come and get inside the scales, they close the scales, go to water, and there open the scales.

S.FORM: nu setjavaw anga azua mareka sasiq, gaɫuin anga nimadju a keman, aya.
S.PHON: nu səcavaw aŋa azua marəka sasiq, gaʎuin aŋa nimaɉu a kəman, aja.
S.FORM: nu setjavaw anga azua mareka sasiq, galjuin anga nimadju a keman, aya.
S.PHON: nu səʦavaw aŋa azua marəka sasiq, gaʎuin aŋa nimaɉu a kəman, aja.
S.TRANSL: When the ants float to the surface, they eat them at their leisure.

S.FORM: aitsu a vutjuɫ nusa qam sanguaq aravats, aya.
S.PHON: aiʦu a vucuʎ nusa qam saŋuaq aravaʦ, aja.
S.FORM: aicu a vutjulj nusa qam sanguaq aravac, aya.
S.PHON: aiʦu a vuʦuʎ nusa qam saŋuaq aravaʦ, aja.
S.TRANSL: Pangolin meat is said to be very tasty;

S.FORM: ɫakua anema tja keɫang nu ika tjen a nakilingaw anga keman, saka kemɫang itjen tu namakuda a pasa sanguaq?
S.PHON: ʎakua anəma ca kəʎaŋ nu ika cən a nakiɭiŋaw aŋa kəman, saka kəmʎaŋ icən tu namakuda a pasa saŋuaq
S.FORM: ljakua anema tja keljang nu ika tjen a nakilingaw anga keman, saka kemljang itjen tu namakuda a pasa sanguaq?
S.PHON: ʎakua anəma ʦa kəʎaŋ nu ika ʦən a nakiɭiŋaw aŋa kəman, saka kəmʎaŋ iʦən tu namakuda a pasa saŋuaq
S.TRANSL: but how can we know if we haven’t had the experience of eating it and finding out how tasty it is?

S.FORM: a ti nakeman anga mana ki kemɫang.
S.PHON: a ti nakəman aŋa mana ki kəmʎaŋ.
S.FORM: a ti nakeman anga mana ki kemljang.
S.PHON: a ti nakəman aŋa mana ki kəmʎaŋ.
S.TRANSL: Those who have eaten it know.
